top_stories.label: Top Stories
top_stories.ai_summary: The Middle East dominates: Trump says a US–Iran deal to end the war will be signed today, June 14, though Tehran is questioning the timing and protesters gathered outside Iran's foreign ministry. Fighting hasn't stopped — Israel is striking south and east Lebanon with broad evacuation warnings, and Ukraine hit a Russian Black Sea port overnight. Elsewhere, Trump heads to the G-7, the US says it killed a Tren de Aragua gang leader in Venezuela, and Washington ordered Anthropic to bar foreign-national access to its top AI models.

stories.headline: Trump says US–Iran deal to end war will be signed June 14; Tehran questions timing
stories.summary: President Trump said an agreement to end the US–Iran war would be signed on Sunday, June 14, while Iranian officials publicly cast doubt on the timing. The framing of the deal remains contested between Washington and Tehran.

stories.headline: Lebanon reports strikes on south and east as Israel issues broad evacuation warnings
stories.summary: Lebanese authorities reported Israeli air strikes on southern and eastern Lebanon after Israel issued wide evacuation warnings. Hezbollah said it confronted Israeli troops advancing in the south; casualty claims have not been independently verified.

stories.headline: Dozens protest peace deal outside Iran's foreign ministry
stories.summary: Iranian media reported that dozens gathered outside the foreign ministry in Tehran to protest the proposed deal with the United States, underscoring domestic opposition as the agreement is finalised.

stories.headline: Ukraine drone strike sparks fire at Russian port of Temryuk, one killed
stories.summary: A Ukrainian drone strike on the Russian Black Sea port of Temryuk in Krasnodar killed one person and sparked a fire, the regional governor said, as Kyiv steps up long-range strikes on Russian ports and energy sites.

stories.headline: Trump to meet Middle East leaders and attend Ukraine session at G-7
stories.summary: US officials said President Trump will meet Middle East leaders and attend a session on Ukraine at the G-7 summit, as Washington pushes its diplomacy on both conflicts simultaneously.

stories.headline: US says operation in Venezuela killed Tren de Aragua gang leader
stories.summary: President Trump said a US operation killed the leader of the Tren de Aragua gang in Venezuela. Independent confirmation of the strike's details was limited at the time of reporting.

stories.headline: Trump's name removed from Kennedy Center after court order
stories.summary: Trump's name was removed from the Kennedy Center in a predawn operation after a judge declined to halt the change, capping a legal back-and-forth over the renaming.

stories.headline: Iran sets funeral for late Supreme Leader Khamenei; burial on July 9
stories.summary: Iranian state TV said the funeral for the late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei will begin July 4, with burial on July 9. The announcement follows the leadership transition in Tehran during the conflict with the US.

stories.headline: US orders Anthropic to bar foreign-national access to Fable, Mythos models
stories.summary: The US government asked Anthropic to suspend access to its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 AI models for foreign nationals, citing national-security concerns. Anthropic moved to restrict the tools in response.

stories.headline: US Justice Department approves Warner Bros' $111bn sale to Paramount
stories.summary: The US Justice Department approved the roughly $111 billion sale of Warner Bros to Paramount, clearing one of the largest media mergers in recent years.

stories.headline: 2026 World Cup kicks off; Qatar snatch late draw with Switzerland
stories.summary: The expanded 2026 World Cup got underway, with Qatar scoring late to clinch a draw against Switzerland in the opener as host-nation matches drew large crowds.

stories.headline: Switzerland votes on proposal to cap population at 10 million
stories.summary: Swiss voters went to the polls on a proposal to cap the country's population at 10 million, a referendum that has reignited debate over immigration and growth.

singapore.label: Singapore
singapore.ai_summary: A busy local Sunday: LTA is warning drivers against using clips to keep seat belts slack, PSA paid a fine over a 2017 terminal worker's death while appealing the verdict, and Minister Sim Ann set out Singapore's targeted approach to online regulation. On the lighter side, "forward deployed engineers" are the hot new AI hire, and a Thai photographer was fined S$15,000 for droning over SAFTI. Expect thundery showers with highs around 34°C; COE stays pricey, with Cat A and Cat B both near S$127k.

stories.headline: LTA warns against using clips to keep seat belts slack
stories.summary: The Land Transport Authority warned motorists against the practice of using clips and other devices to keep seat belts slack, saying it defeats the belt's safety function and can be an offence.

stories.headline: PSA pays fine over 2017 Keppel Terminal worker death, but appeals verdict
stories.summary: PSA paid a fine over a worker's death at Keppel Terminal in 2017 but is appealing the guilty verdict and sentence, continuing a long-running workplace-safety case.

stories.headline: Singapore takes targeted approach to online regulation, says Sim Ann
stories.summary: Minister of State Sim Ann said Singapore takes a targeted approach to online regulation, focused on national interest rather than broad content controls, in remarks on the country's digital-governance stance.

stories.headline: Pasir Ris-Changi residents to get discounted food and services under new programme
stories.summary: A new programme will give Pasir Ris-Changi residents access to discounted food and services, aimed at easing cost-of-living pressures in the area.

stories.headline: Singapore deeply saddened by death of Thai Princess Bajrakitiyabha
stories.summary: The Singapore government said it was deeply saddened by the death of Thailand's Princess Bajrakitiyabha Mahidol, extending condolences as mourners gathered in Bangkok.

stories.headline: Photographer fined S$15,000 for flying drone over SAFTI, Pasir Laba Camp
stories.summary: A freelance photographer was fined S$15,000 after flying a drone without a permit and capturing footage of SAFTI Military Institute and Pasir Laba Camp, a case that drew wide local attention.

stories.headline: The hottest new AI job: 'forward deployed engineers' in demand in Singapore
stories.summary: Singapore employers are increasingly hiring "forward deployed engineers" — technical staff who embed with customers to deploy AI tools — as one of the hottest new roles in the local tech market.

stories.headline: Investors searching for former SEA Games ice hockey player over $9m loan
stories.summary: Investors are trying to track down a former SEA Games ice hockey player over a $9 million loan, according to court-related reporting on the dispute.

wars.label: Wars & Conflicts
wars.ai_summary: The US–Iran war may be winding down on paper — Trump says a deal will be signed June 14 — but strikes continued through the week and Iran is publicly hedging on the timing. Israel has widened its air campaign in Lebanon with broad evacuation warnings, and Hezbollah says it clashed with advancing Israeli troops. In Ukraine, the pattern holds: Russian drone-and-missile barrages on Ukrainian cities, answered by Kyiv's deepening long-range strikes on Russian ports and energy infrastructure.

theatres.name: US–Iran Conflict (Strait of Hormuz)
theatres.status: Trump says a deal to end the war will be signed June 14; Iran questions the timing as strikes continue and protesters oppose the agreement in Tehran.

timeline.event: Iran and Israel say they will pause strikes but warn of retaliation if a ceasefire is breached again.
US strikes Iran in response to the downing of a US military helicopter near the Strait of Hormuz.
US and Iran exchange strikes across the Middle East for a second day; a US strike on an oil tanker kills three Indian sailors.
Israel carries out air strikes on Lebanon as Iran says a deal with the US is near; Iranian state TV sets the late Supreme Leader Khamenei's funeral for July 4, burial July 9.
Trump says the deal will be signed today; Iran questions the timing and dozens protest outside the foreign ministry in Tehran.

theatres.name: Israel–Lebanon (Hezbollah)
theatres.status: Israel striking south and east Lebanon with broad evacuation warnings; Hezbollah says it confronted advancing Israeli troops. Casualty claims not independently verified.

timeline.event: Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon kill 17, according to local reports.
Israel carries out air strikes on Lebanon, Lebanese state media says.
Lebanon reports strikes on the south and east as Israel issues broad evacuation warnings; Hezbollah says it confronted Israeli troops advancing in the south.

theatres.name: Russia–Ukraine War
theatres.status: Russia continues drone-and-missile barrages on Ukrainian cities; Ukraine deepens long-range strikes on Russian ports and energy infrastructure.

timeline.event: Russia's fuel crisis intensifies as Ukraine steps up strikes on occupied territories, the BBC reports.
Ukraine says its missiles hit a military plant deep inside Russia.
Ukrainian officials report Russian overnight drone-and-missile attacks across the country, with casualties reported in several regions.
A Ukrainian drone strike sparks a fire at Russia's Temryuk port in Krasnodar, killing one, the regional governor says.

outbreaks.label: Outbreaks
outbreaks.ai_summary: The dominant outbreak is Bundibugyo Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda, which WHO says is climbing fast — 695 confirmed cases and 138 deaths as of its June 13 update, with the recent jump partly due to a backlog of samples now being tested. The earlier hantavirus cluster tied to the cruise ship M/V Hondius (13 cases, 3 deaths) has stayed quiet, with no new WHO update since late May. Ebola is the one to watch.

outbreaks.name: Bundibugyo Ebola — DR Congo & Uganda
outbreaks.status: Rising rapidly in DR Congo; cross-border cases in Uganda. Active.
outbreaks.scope_note: Confirmed cases only, DRC + Uganda combined (DRC 676 cases/136 deaths; Uganda 19 cases/2 deaths); at least 37 recovered. Per WHO DON607 (DRC data as of 10 Jun, Uganda 11 Jun); the recent rise partly reflects testing of a backlog of samples.

outbreaks.name: Hantavirus cluster — M/V Hondius cruise ship
outbreaks.status: No new WHO update since 28 May; cluster appears contained. Off-ship risk low.
outbreaks.scope_note: 11 confirmed + 2 probable cases, 3 deaths among passengers/crew of the Netherlands-flagged M/V Hondius (nationalities incl. Canada, Netherlands, Spain). Per WHO DON604; figures are now over two weeks old with no newer DON — treat as stale/contained.

stories.headline: WHO: Bundibugyo Ebola cases climb to 695 in DR Congo and Uganda
stories.summary: In its June 13 Disease Outbreak News, WHO reported 695 confirmed Bundibugyo virus cases and 138 deaths across the DRC and Uganda, with 161 additional confirmed cases in the DRC since June 8 — a rise partly driven by testing of previously collected samples.

cybersecurity.label: CyberSecurity
cybersecurity.ai_summary: Critical, unauthenticated RCE bugs lead the cycle: Splunk Enterprise (CVE-2026-20253, 9.8) and a maxed-out Ivanti Sentry flaw (CVE-2026-10520, 10.0) that CISA ordered patched within days, plus an actively exploited Oracle PeopleSoft zero-day (CVE-2026-35273) that ShinyHunters used to hit universities. Supply-chain and stealth campaigns are the other theme — 400+ hijacked Arch Linux AUR packages and a decade-long China-linked Linux login backdoor. A run of data breaches (Novo Nordisk, a French government messenger, a Japanese energy firm) rounds out the week.

stories.headline: Critical Splunk Enterprise flaw lets attackers run code without authentication
stories.summary: An unauthenticated flaw in Splunk Enterprise's PostgreSQL sidecar service lets attackers write arbitrary files and chain to remote code execution; AWS-hosted instances are vulnerable by default. Patched in 10.2.4 / 10.0.7; no public exploitation reported yet.

stories.headline: ShinyHunters exploits Oracle PeopleSoft zero-day to breach universities
stories.summary: The ShinyHunters extortion group is actively exploiting an unauthenticated RCE zero-day in Oracle PeopleSoft, allegedly breaching about 300 instances across 100+ organisations — most in US higher education, per Mandiant. CISA added it to the KEV catalog; Oracle has issued mitigations.
stories.source: The Hacker News, BleepingComputer, CISA KEV

stories.headline: CISA orders feds to patch actively exploited Ivanti Sentry flaw within days
stories.summary: CISA gave federal agencies a short deadline to patch an actively exploited OS command-injection flaw in Ivanti Sentry, rated a maximum CVSS 10.0. The bug is in the KEV catalog and enables full device compromise.

stories.headline: Over 400 Arch Linux AUR packages hijacked to push infostealer and rootkit
stories.summary: Attackers compromised more than 400 packages in the Arch Linux User Repository (AUR) to deploy an infostealer and an eBPF-based rootkit on users' machines, a significant open-source supply-chain compromise.

stories.headline: China-linked hackers backdoored Linux login software to hide for nearly a decade
stories.summary: Researchers say a China-linked group hijacked the authentication flow in Linux login software to maintain covert access to an isolated network for almost ten years, an unusually long-running stealth intrusion.

stories.headline: Novo Nordisk discloses breach of clinical trials data
stories.summary: Pharmaceutical giant Novo Nordisk disclosed a security breach affecting clinical-trials data, raising concerns about exposure of sensitive medical research information.

stories.headline: Tchap messenger breach hits over 73,000 French government accounts
stories.summary: France said a breach of its Tchap secure messenger affected more than 73,000 government employee accounts, exposing data from the state's in-house communications platform.

stories.headline: Japanese energy firm loses drive holding data of 10.9 million clients
stories.summary: A Japanese energy company reported losing a storage drive containing the personal data of about 10.9 million customers, a large exposure of client information.

stories.headline: phpBB forum fixes authentication-bypass bug that lurked for a decade
stories.summary: The phpBB project patched an authentication-bypass vulnerability that had gone unnoticed for around ten years, a serious flaw for the many community forums that run the software.

stories.headline: Agentjacking attack tricks AI coding agents into running malicious code
stories.summary: Researchers detailed an "agentjacking" technique that manipulates AI coding agents into executing attacker-supplied code, highlighting prompt-injection-style risks as developers adopt autonomous coding tools.

stories.headline: LangGraph flaw chain exposes self-hosted AI agents to remote code execution
stories.summary: A chain of flaws in LangGraph can expose self-hosted AI agent deployments to remote code execution, putting organisations running their own agent frameworks at risk.

stories.headline: Google sues Chinese smishing network accused of using Gemini AI in phishing
stories.summary: Google filed suit against a China-based smishing network it accuses of abusing its Gemini AI to scale text-message phishing campaigns, part of a wider push against AI-assisted fraud operations.

stories.headline: INTERPOL takes down Sniper Dz phishing platform, arrests administrator
stories.summary: An INTERPOL-coordinated operation dismantled the Sniper Dz phishing-as-a-service platform and arrested its administrator, disrupting infrastructure used to run large-scale credential-theft campaigns.

stories.headline: The Gentlemen ransomware claims 478 victims and can spread like a worm
stories.summary: The Gentlemen ransomware operation claims 478 victims and includes worm-like self-propagation, letting it spread automatically across networks once it gains a foothold.

stories.headline: GitHub to disable npm install scripts by default to curb supply-chain attacks
stories.summary: GitHub said it will disable npm install scripts by default, removing a common vector that malicious packages use to auto-execute code during installation — a notable hardening of the JavaScript ecosystem.
